1→# Database Models for Mochi Donut - SQLAlchemy 2.0 Async
     2→"""
     3→Production-ready SQLAlchemy 2.0 models with async patterns for the Mochi Donut
     4→spaced repetition learning system. Follows FastAPI best practices and includes
     5→proper indexing for performance optimization.
     6→"""
     7→
     8→from datetime import datetime
     9→from enum import Enum as PyEnum
    10→from typing import List, Optional
    11→import uuid
    12→
    13→from sqlalchemy import (
    14→    JSON, Boolean, DateTime, Enum, Float, ForeignKey, Index, String, Text,
    15→    UniqueConstraint, func
    16→)
    17→from sqlalchemy.dialects.postgresql import UUID
    18→from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncAttrs
    19→from sqlalchemy.orm import DeclarativeBase, Mapped, mapped_column, relationship
    20→
    21→
    22→class Base(AsyncAttrs, DeclarativeBase):
    23→    """Base class for all database models with async support."""
    24→    pass
    25→
    26→
    27→# Enums for type safety
    28→class SourceType(PyEnum):
    29→    """Content source types supported by the system."""
    30→    WEB = "web"
    31→    PDF = "pdf"
    32→    YOUTUBE = "youtube"
    33→    NOTION = "notion"
    34→    RAINDROP = "raindrop"
    35→    MARKDOWN = "markdown"
    36→
    37→
    38→class PromptType(PyEnum):
    39→    """Types of prompts following Andy Matuschak's patterns."""
    40→    FACTUAL = "factual"
    41→    PROCEDURAL = "procedural"
    42→    CONCEPTUAL = "conceptual"
    43→    OPEN_LIST = "open_list"
    44→    CLOZE_DELETION = "cloze_deletion"
    45→
    46→
    47→class ProcessingStatus(PyEnum):
    48→    """Status of content processing through the system."""
    49→    PENDING = "pending"
    50→    PROCESSING = "processing"
    51→    COMPLETED = "completed"
    52→    FAILED = "failed"
    53→    SKIPPED = "skipped"
    54→
    55→
    56→class QualityMetricType(PyEnum):
    57→    """Quality metrics for prompt evaluation."""
    58→    FOCUS_SPECIFICITY = "focus_specificity"
    59→    PRECISION_CLARITY = "precision_clarity"
    60→    COGNITIVE_LOAD = "cognitive_load"
    61→    RETRIEVAL_PRACTICE = "retrieval_practice"
    62→    OVERALL_QUALITY = "overall_quality"
    63→
    64→
    65→class AgentType(PyEnum):
    66→    """Types of AI agents in the system."""
    67→    ORCHESTRATOR = "orchestrator"
    68→    CONTENT_ANALYSIS = "content_analysis"
    69→    PROMPT_GENERATION = "prompt_generation"
    70→    QUALITY_REVIEW = "quality_review"
    71→    REFINEMENT = "refinement"
    72→
    73→
    74→# Core Models
    75→
    76→class Content(Base):
    77→    """
    78→    Stores processed content from various sources with unified markdown format.
    79→    Includes metadata for semantic search and duplicate detection.
    80→    """
    81→    __tablename__ = "contents"
    82→
    83→    # Primary key and identification
    84→    id: Mapped[uuid.UUID] = mapped_column(
    85→        UUID(as_uuid=True),
    86→        primary_key=True,
    87→        default=uuid.uuid4,
    88→        index=True
    89→    )
    90→
    91→    # Source information
    92→    source_url: Mapped[Optional[str]] = mapped_column(String(2048), nullable=True)
    93→    source_type: Mapped[SourceType] = mapped_column(Enum(SourceType), nullable=False)
    94→    title: Mapped[Optional[str]] = mapped_column(String(500), nullable=True)
    95→    author: Mapped[Optional[str]] = mapped_column(String(255), nullable=True)
    96→
    97→    # Content storage
    98→    raw_text: Mapped[Optional[str]] = mapped_column(Text, nullable=True)
    99→    markdown_content: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(Text, nullable=False)
   100→    content_hash: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(64), nullable=False, unique=True)
   101→
   102→    # Vector database integration
   103→    chroma_collection: Mapped[Optional[str]] = mapped_column(String(255), nullable=True)
   104→    chroma_document_id: Mapped[Optional[str]] = mapped_column(String(255), nullable=True)
   105→
   106→    # Processing metadata
   107→    word_count: Mapped[Optional[int]] = mapped_column(nullable=True)
   108→    estimated_reading_time: Mapped[Optional[int]] = mapped_column(nullable=True)  # minutes
   109→    processing_status: Mapped[ProcessingStatus] = mapped_column(
   110→        Enum(ProcessingStatus),
   111→        default=ProcessingStatus.PENDING
   112→    )
   113→
   114→    # Metadata and configuration
   115→    content_metadata: Mapped[Optional[dict]] = mapped_column(JSON, nullable=True)
   116→    processing_config: Mapped[Optional[dict]] = mapped_column(JSON, nullable=True)
   117→
   118→    # Timestamps
   119→    created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(
   120→        DateTime(timezone=True),
   121→        server_default=func.now()
   122→    )
   123→    updated_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(
   124→        DateTime(timezone=True),
   125→        server_default=func.now(),
   126→        onupdate=func.now()
   127→    )
   128→    processed_at: Mapped[Optional[datetime]] = mapped_column(
   129→        DateTime(timezone=True),
   130→        nullable=True
   131→    )
   132→
   133→    # Relationships
   134→    prompts: Mapped[List["Prompt"]] = relationship(
   135→        "Prompt",
   136→        back_populates="content",
   137→        cascade="all, delete-orphan"
   138→    )
   139→    agent_executions: Mapped[List["AgentExecution"]] = relationship(
   140→        "AgentExecution",
   141→        back_populates="content",
   142→        cascade="all, delete-orphan"
   143→    )
   144→
   145→    # Indexes for performance
   146→    __table_args__ = (
   147→        Index("ix_content_source_type", "source_type"),
   148→        Index("ix_content_status", "processing_status"),
   149→        Index("ix_content_created_at", "created_at"),
   150→        Index("ix_content_hash", "content_hash"),
   151→        Index("ix_content_chroma", "chroma_collection", "chroma_document_id"),
   152→    )
   153→
   154→
   155→class Prompt(Base):
   156→    """
   157→    Generated flashcard prompts with quality metrics and versioning.
   158→    Tracks relationship to source content and Mochi integration.
   159→    """
   160→    __tablename__ = "prompts"
   161→
   162→    # Primary key
   163→    id: Mapped[uuid.UUID] = mapped_column(
   164→        UUID(as_uuid=True),
   165→        primary_key=True,
   166→        default=uuid.uuid4,
   167→        index=True
   168→    )
   169→
   170→    # Content relationship
   171→    content_id: Mapped[uuid.UUID] = mapped_column(
   172→        UUID(as_uuid=True),
   173→        ForeignKey("contents.id", ondelete="CASCADE"),
   174→        nullable=False
   175→    )
   176→
   177→    # Prompt content
   178→    question: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(Text, nullable=False)

   181→
   182→    # Quality and confidence
   183→    confidence_score: Mapped[Optional[float]] = mapped_column(Float, nullable=True)

   185→
   186→    # Versioning and editing
   187→    version: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(default=1, nullable=False)
   188→    is_edited: Mapped[bool] = mapped_column(Boolean, default=False)
   189→    edit_reason: Mapped[Optional[str]] = mapped_column(String(500), nullable=True)
   190→
   191→    # Mochi integration
   192→    mochi_card_id: Mapped[Optional[str]] = mapped_column(String(255), nullable=True)

   194→    mochi_status: Mapped[Optional[str]] = mapped_column(String(50), nullable=True)
   195→
   196→    # Context and metadata
   197→    source_context: Mapped[Optional[str]] = mapped_column(Text, nullable=True)
   198→    tags: Mapped[Optional[List[str]]] = mapped_column(JSON, nullable=True)
   199→    prompt_metadata: Mapped[Optional[dict]] = mapped_column(JSON, nullable=True)
   200→
   201→    # Timestamps
   202→    created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(
   203→        DateTime(timezone=True),
   204→        server_default=func.now()
   205→    )
   206→    updated_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(
   207→        DateTime(timezone=True),

   209→        onupdate=func.now()
   210→    )
   211→    edited_at: Mapped[Optional[datetime]] = mapped_column(
   212→        DateTime(timezone=True),
   213→        nullable=True
   214→    )
   215→    sent_to_mochi_at: Mapped[Optional[datetime]] = mapped_column(
   216→        DateTime(timezone=True),
   217→        nullable=True
   218→    )
   219→
   220→    # Relationships
   221→    content: Mapped["Content"] = relationship("Content", back_populates="prompts")
   222→    quality_metrics: Mapped[List["QualityMetric"]] = relationship(
   223→        "QualityMetric",
   224→        back_populates="prompt",
   225→        cascade="all, delete-orphan"
   226→    )
   227→    user_interactions: Mapped[List["UserInteraction"]] = relationship(
   228→        "UserInteraction",
   229→        back_populates="prompt",
   230→        cascade="all, delete-orphan"
   231→    )
   232→
   233→    # Indexes for performance
   234→    __table_args__ = (
   235→        Index("ix_prompt_content_id", "content_id"),
   236→        Index("ix_prompt_type", "prompt_type"),
   237→        Index("ix_prompt_confidence", "confidence_score"),
   238→        Index("ix_prompt_mochi_card", "mochi_card_id"),
   239→        Index("ix_prompt_created_at", "created_at"),
   240→        Index("ix_prompt_content_type", "content_id", "prompt_type"),
   241→    )

   244→class QualityMetric(Base):
   245→    """
   246→    Quality assessment metrics for prompts based on Andy Matuschak's principles.

1→# Alembic Environment Configuration - Async Support
     2→"""
     3→Alembic environment configuration with async SQLAlchemy 2.0 support.
     4→Handles both offline and online migration modes with proper async patterns.
     5→"""
     6→
     7→import asyncio
     8→import os
     9→from logging.config import fileConfig
    10→from typing import Optional
    11→
    12→from sqlalchemy import engine_from_config, pool
    13→from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncEngine, create_async_engine
    14→from alembic import context
    15→
    16→# Import your models here to ensure they're registered with metadata
    17→from app.db.models import Base
    18→from app.db.config import get_database_settings
    19→
    20→# this is the Alembic Config object, which provides
    21→# access to the values within the .ini file in use.
    22→config = context.config
    23→
    24→# Interpret the config file for Python logging.
    25→# This line sets up loggers basically.
    26→if config.config_file_name is not None:
    27→    fileConfig(config.config_file_name)
    28→
    29→# add your model's MetaData object here
    30→# for 'autogenerate' support
    31→target_metadata = Base.metadata
    32→
    33→# other values from the config, defined by the needs of env.py,
    34→# can be acquired:
    35→# my_important_option = config.get_main_option("my_important_option")
    36→# ... etc.
    37→
    38→
    39→def get_database_url() -> str:
    40→    """
    41→    Get database URL from environment or configuration.
    42→
    43→    Returns:
    44→        Database URL string
    45→    """
    46→    # Try environment variable first
    47→    url = os.getenv("DATABASE_URL")
    48→    if url:
    49→        return url
    50→
    51→    # Fall back to settings
    52→    settings = get_database_settings()
    53→    return settings.database_url
    54→
    55→
    56→def run_migrations_offline() -> None:
    57→    """
    58→    Run migrations in 'offline' mode.
    59→
    60→    This configures the context with just a URL
    61→    and not an Engine, though an Engine is acceptable
    62→    here as well. By skipping the Engine creation
    63→    we don't even need a DBAPI to be available.
    64→
    65→    Calls to context.execute() here emit the given string to the
    66→    script output.
    67→    """
    68→    url = get_database_url()
    69→
    70→    # Remove async driver prefix for offline mode
    71→    if url.startswith("postgresql+asyncpg://"):
    72→        url = url.replace("postgresql+asyncpg://", "postgresql://")
    73→    elif url.startswith("sqlite+aiosqlite://"):
    74→        url = url.replace("sqlite+aiosqlite://", "sqlite://")
    75→
    76→    context.configure(
    77→        url=url,
    78→        target_metadata=target_metadata,
    79→        literal_binds=True,
    80→        dialect_opts={"paramstyle": "named"},
    81→        compare_type=True,
    82→        compare_server_default=True,
    83→        render_as_batch=True,  # For SQLite compatibility
    84→    )
    85→
    86→    with context.begin_transaction():
    87→        context.run_migrations()
    88→
    89→
    90→def do_run_migrations(connection) -> None:
    91→    """
    92→    Run migrations with the given connection.
    93→
    94→    Args:
    95→        connection: Database connection
    96→    """
    97→    context.configure(
    98→        connection=connection,
    99→        target_metadata=target_metadata,
   100→        compare_type=True,
   101→        compare_server_default=True,
   102→        render_as_batch=True,  # For SQLite compatibility
   103→    )
   104→
   105→    with context.begin_transaction():
   106→        context.run_migrations()
   107→
   108→
   109→async def run_async_migrations() -> None:
   110→    """
   111→    Run migrations in async mode.
   112→
   113→    Creates an async engine and runs migrations within
   114→    an async context.
   115→    """
   116→    database_url = get_database_url()
   117→
   118→    # Create async engine
   119→    connectable = create_async_engine(
   120→        database_url,
   121→        echo=False,
   122→        future=True,
   123→    )
   124→
   125→    async with connectable.connect() as connection:
   126→        await connection.run_sync(do_run_migrations)
   127→
   128→    await connectable.dispose()
   129→
   130→
   131→def run_migrations_online() -> None:
   132→    """
   133→    Run migrations in 'online' mode.
   134→
   135→    In this scenario we need to create an Engine
   136→    and associate a connection with the context.
   137→    """
   138→    # Check if we're in async mode
   139→    database_url = get_database_url()
   140→
   141→    if any(driver in database_url for driver in ["asyncpg", "aiosqlite"]):
   142→        # Async mode
   143→        asyncio.run(run_async_migrations())
   144→    else:
   145→        # Sync mode fallback
   146→        connectable = engine_from_config(
   147→            config.get_section(config.config_ini_section),
   148→            prefix="sqlalchemy.",
   149→            poolclass=pool.NullPool,
   150→        )
   151→
   152→        with connectable.connect() as connection:
   153→            do_run_migrations(connection)
   154→
   155→
   156→if context.is_offline_mode():
   157→    run_migrations_offline()
   158→else:
   159→    run_migrations_online()

   108→class DatabaseManager:
   109→    """
   110→    Database manager for async SQLAlchemy operations.
   111→    Handles engine creation, session management, and connection lifecycle.
   112→    """
   113→
   114→    def __init__(self, settings: Optional[DatabaseSettings] = None):
   115→        """
   116→        Initialize database manager with settings.
   117→
   118→        Args:
   119→            settings: Optional database settings (defaults to global settings)
   120→        """
   121→        self.settings = settings or get_database_settings()
   122→        self._engine: Optional[AsyncEngine] = None
   123→        self._session_maker: Optional[async_sessionmaker] = None
   124→
   125→    @property
   126→    def engine(self) -> AsyncEngine:
   127→        """
   128→        Get or create the async database engine.
   129→
   130→        Returns:
   131→            Configured async SQLAlchemy engine
   132→        """
   133→        if self._engine is None:
   134→            database_url = self.settings.database_url

   136→                database_url = self.settings.test_database_url
   137→
   138→            engine_kwargs = self.settings.get_engine_kwargs()
   139→            self._engine = create_async_engine(database_url, **engine_kwargs)
   140→
   141→        return self._engine
   142→
   143→    @property

   145→        """

   147→
   148→        Returns:
   149→            Configured async session maker
   150→        """
   151→        if self._session_maker is None:
   152→            self._session_maker = async_sessionmaker(
   153→                bind=self.engine,
   154→                class_=AsyncSession,
   155→                expire_on_commit=self.settings.expire_on_commit,
   156→                autoflush=True,
   157→                autocommit=False,
   158→            )
   159→
   160→        return self._session_maker

   165→        Should only be used in development or for testing.
   166→        """
   167→        from app.db.models import Base
   168→
   169→        async with self.engine.begin() as conn:
   170→            await conn.run_sync(Base.metadata.create_all)

   172→    async def drop_all_tables(self) -> None:
   173→        """

   180→        from app.db.models import Base

   182→        async with self.engine.begin() as conn:

   192→        return self.session_maker()

   195→        """
   196→        Close the database engine and all connections.
   197→        """
   198→        if self._engine:

class AgentExecution(Base):
    """
    Tracks AI agent processing steps and performance for debugging and optimization.
    Records the complete execution chain for content processing.

    Enhanced for Claude SDK: Tracks subagent invocations, parent-child relationships,
    and Claude SDK-specific execution patterns.
    """
    __tablename__ = "agent_executions"

    # Primary key
    id: Mapped[uuid.UUID] = mapped_column(
        UUID(as_uuid=True),
        primary_key=True,
        default=uuid.uuid4
    )

    # Content relationship
    content_id: Mapped[Optional[uuid.UUID]] = mapped_column(
        UUID(as_uuid=True),
        ForeignKey("contents.id", ondelete="CASCADE"),
        nullable=True
    )

    # Execution details
    agent_type: Mapped[AgentType] = mapped_column(Enum(AgentType), nullable=False)
    execution_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(255), nullable=False)  # For grouping
    step_number: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(nullable=False)
    status: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(50), nullable=False)

    # Claude SDK subagent tracking
    parent_execution_id: Mapped[Optional[str]] = mapped_column(String(255), nullable=True)
    subagent_type: Mapped[Optional[str]] = mapped_column(String(100), nullable=True)
    is_subagent: Mapped[bool] = mapped_column(Boolean, default=False)

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Quick test script to verify database models and async session work correctly.
Tests basic CRUD operations on Content and AgentExecution models.
"""

import asyncio
import hashlib
from datetime import datetime

from app.db.config import get_database_manager
from app.db.models import (
    Content,
    SourceType,
    ProcessingStatus,
    AgentExecution,
    AgentType,
)


async def test_database():
    """Test database initialization and basic operations."""
    print("🔧 Testing Database Configuration...")

    # Get database manager
    db_manager = get_database_manager()

    # Test health check
    print("Testing database health check...")
    is_healthy = await db_manager.health_check()
    if is_healthy:
        print("✅ Database health check passed")
    else:
        print("❌ Database health check failed")
        return False

    # Test session creation and CRUD operations
    print("\nTesting async session and CRUD operations...")
    async with db_manager.session_maker() as session:
        try:
            # Create a test Content record
            print("Creating test Content record...")
            test_content = Content(
                source_type=SourceType.WEB,
                title="Test Article",
                author="Test Author",
                markdown_content="# Test Content\n\nThis is test markdown content.",
                content_hash=hashlib.sha256(b"test content").hexdigest(),
                word_count=100,
                estimated_reading_time=1,
                processing_status=ProcessingStatus.COMPLETED,
            )

            session.add(test_content)
            await session.flush()  # Get the ID
            print(f"✅ Created Content with ID: {test_content.id}")

            # Create a test AgentExecution record with Claude SDK fields
            print("\nCreating test AgentExecution record with Claude SDK fields...")
            test_execution = AgentExecution(
                content_id=test_content.id,
                agent_type=AgentType.PLANNER,
                execution_id="test-exec-001",
                step_number=1,
                status="completed",
                model_used="claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929",
                input_tokens=1000,
                output_tokens=500,
                execution_time_ms=2500,
                cost_usd=0.015,
                # Claude SDK-specific fields
                parent_execution_id=None,
                subagent_type="planner",
                is_subagent=False,
            )

            session.add(test_execution)
            await session.flush()
            print(f"✅ Created AgentExecution with ID: {test_execution.id}")

            # Create a child subagent execution
            print("\nCreating child subagent execution...")
            child_execution = AgentExecution(
                content_id=test_content.id,
                agent_type=AgentType.CODER,
                execution_id="test-exec-002",
                step_number=2,
                status="completed",
                model_used="claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929",
                input_tokens=2000,
                output_tokens=1000,
                execution_time_ms=5000,
                cost_usd=0.030,
                # Claude SDK-specific fields
                parent_execution_id="test-exec-001",
                subagent_type="coder",
                is_subagent=True,
            )

            session.add(child_execution)
            await session.commit()
            print(f"✅ Created child AgentExecution with ID: {child_execution.id}")

            # Query back the records
            print("\nQuerying records back...")
            from sqlalchemy import select

            result = await session.execute(
                select(Content).where(Content.id == test_content.id)
            )
            content = result.scalar_one_or_none()

            if content:
                print(f"✅ Retrieved Content: {content.title}")
                print(f"   - Word count: {content.word_count}")
                print(f"   - Status: {content.processing_status.value}")

            # Query agent executions with subagent filtering
            result = await session.execute(
                select(AgentExecution)
                .where(AgentExecution.content_id == test_content.id)
                .order_by(AgentExecution.step_number)
            )
            executions = result.scalars().all()

            print(f"\n✅ Retrieved {len(executions)} AgentExecutions:")
            for exec in executions:
                print(f"   - {exec.agent_type.value}: step {exec.step_number}")
                print(f"     • Subagent: {exec.is_subagent}")
                print(f"     • Subagent type: {exec.subagent_type}")
                print(f"     • Parent: {exec.parent_execution_id}")
                print(f"     • Tokens: {exec.input_tokens} in / {exec.output_tokens} out")

            print("\n✅ All database tests passed!")
            return True

        except Exception as e:
            print(f"❌ Database test failed: {e}")
            import traceback

            traceback.print_exc()
            await session.rollback()
            return False

    await db_manager.close()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    success = asyncio.run(test_database())
    exit(0 if success else 1)
